metadata.notes: Static seed data from provided Australia spreadsheets plus AU meal deals and KFC AU menu rows. Some deal/KFC rows only have price+kJ because public sources did not provide serve grams/protein. McSmart Meal rows use user-corrected Australian option/drink combinations and exclude McDouble. User edits are stored in localStorage and can be exported/imported as JSON.
metadata.audit: Expanded meal/deal coverage for Subway, GYG, McDonald's and KFC. Removed Subway FitChips, the $8 Footlong Sub of the Day promo, and invalid McDouble McSmart row. McSmart rows now match the user-provided non-negotiable AU components/options/drinks. Deal prices vary by store/app/campaign and should be checked against Australian store apps.

notes: FitChips removed after user correction; it is not a dependable current Australian Subway item.
The $8 Footlong Sub of the Day promo row was removed after user correction/request; remaining Subway rows are standard items, kids/menu upgrades or other explicitly labelled deals.
Notes & Method
PRICES: Taken from the attached GYG national menu (the ‘from’ / base price for each item).
GYG prices vary by location, ordering channel (in-store vs delivery apps) and over time, so treat these as a guide.
NUTRITION: From the GYG Allergen, Ingredient & Nutritional guide, ‘current at June 25, 2024’.
Serve size (g), energy (kJ) and protein (g) are taken directly from that guide.
Energy (Cal) is calculated as kJ / 4.184.
FILLING: To compare like-for-like, the standard filling is Grilled Chicken where a choice exists.
Exceptions: Quesadilla = cheese only (its base price); $3 Taco = ground beef (only option);
Tender items = crispy chicken tender; corn-chip/queso/fries sides use the dip/seasoning noted.
Brisket adds ~$1 and other fillings shift weight/energy slightly, so value rankings move a little by filling.
METRICS:
Grams / $  = serve size (g) / price  ->  the literal ‘most food per dollar’ measure (headline).
kJ / $     = energy (kJ) / price      ->  most energy per dollar.
Protein g / $ = protein (g) / price  ->  most protein per dollar.
CAVEAT: Grams/$ rewards heavy, dip- or cheese-laden items (e.g. queso fries, corn chips + guac).
For a balanced, filling meal, look at items that rank well across all three metrics — the Enchilada,
Burrito and Bowl are strong all-rounders; Queso Fries and loaded Corn Chips win pure weight-per-dollar.
All value columns are live Excel formulas, so you can edit any price or serve size and rankings update.
McSmart Meal corrected from user-provided AU structure: fixed Cheeseburger + Small Fries, one allowed food option, and one allowed small drink.
McDouble is not an allowed McSmart option and was removed from McSmart meal rows.
McSmart kJ values use the user's supplied component kJ values. Serve grams/protein are only included where all food components are known; drink grams/protein are excluded.
Used Frugal Feeds AU menu/deal listings for public menu/deal prices where original spreadsheets did not include meal deals. Deal prices can vary by store, app and campaign.

Used kfcmenuprice.au current AU menu listing for KFC item prices and energy kJ. Serve grams/protein were not available in the scraped source, so those fields are left null and rendered as unavailable.
